page: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated CPU virtual server powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. System memory consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 3.91 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 160 GB SSD, and the instance runs under a QEMU hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core scaling. The server achieves a strong rating in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall into the lower 25% tier. Memory read bandwidth is also relatively low, with uncached large block reads at 36.09 GB/sec, though write bandwidth is average at 27.85 GB/sec.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation, the ccx23 is suited for workloads requiring predictable single-threaded performance, such as static web serving and text processing. It is less optimal for highly parallelized tasks, software compilation, or database operations, as evidenced by weak scores in PassMark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. The hardware profile offers a dedicated resource model, ensuring no CPU steal from neighbors, which provides qualitative stability for general-purpose application hosting.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated CPU virtual server based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores, 2 threads per core) running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 160 GB SSD. Operating under a QEMU hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core performance, as demonstrated by its stress-ng single-core score of 2692.67 and high single-threaded bzip3 decompression rates. However, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ccx23 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU stability without extreme multi-threaded demands. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and text processing tasks, while being less optimal for heavy database operations or parallelized software compilation.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 4 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is an AMD EPYC Milan virtual server with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose tasks.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server offering balanced resources and strong single-core performance for stable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs (2 physical cores)
16.0 GB RAM and 160 GB local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Average static web serving throughput of 6.2 GB/sec
Best suited for general-purpose hosting and single-threaded tasks
